transport.h(3) Library Functions Manual transport.h(3)

NAME

transport.h - Transport abstraction layer for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acip_transport_methods_t
Transport method table (virtual function table). struct acip_transport
Transport instance structure.

Enumerations


enum acip_transport_type_t { ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP = 1, ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET = 2, ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC = 3, ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP = 4, ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC = 5 }
Transport type enumeration.

Functions


void acip_transport_destroy (acip_transport_t *transport)
Destroy transport and free all resources. acip_transport_t * acip_tcp_transport_create (const char *name, socket_t sockfd, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create TCP transport from existing socket. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. acip_transport_t * acip_webrtc_transport_create (struct webrtc_peer_connection *peer_conn, struct webrtc_data_channel *data_channel, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)

Detailed Description

Transport abstraction layer for ACIP protocol.

Provides a transport-agnostic interface that allows ACIP protocol handlers to work with any underlying transport (TCP, WebSocket, HTTP, etc.).

Transport Layer Design

Architecture

The transport layer abstracts protocol delivery, allowing the same ACIP protocol handlers to work with multiple underlying transports:

  • TCP (acip_tcp_transport_create): Raw TCP sockets, binary packet streaming
  • WebSocket (acip_websocket_client_transport_create): HTTP upgrade, frame-based
  • WebRTC (acip_webrtc_transport_create): P2P data channels, encryption at transport
  • HTTP (future): Long-polling, REST-style
  • QUIC (future): UDP with congestion control

Design Principles

1.
Protocol code never calls socket()/send()/recv() directly
2.
Same ACIP handlers work with any transport implementation
3.
Each transport handles its own connection state and reliability
4.
Clean separation between protocol logic (acip_*) and transport logic

Packet Framing

Transports handle packet framing transparently:

  • TCP: Uses packet_header_t (magic + type + length + CRC32 + client_id)
  • WebSocket: Wraps packets in WebSocket frames
  • WebRTC: Sends packets directly on data channel

Version Information

  • Transport API Version: 1.0 (stable)
  • Supported ACIP Version: 1.0
  • Release Date: January 2026

DESIGN GOALS:

1.
Protocol code never calls socket()/send()/recv() directly
2.
Same ACIP handlers work with TCP, WebSocket, or any future transport
3.
Transport implementations handle their own connection state
4.
Clean separation between protocol logic and transport logic

USAGE PATTERN: 

// Create TCP transport from existing socket
acip_transport_t *tcp = acip_tcp_transport_create(sockfd, crypto_ctx);
// Send packet with type-specific helper
asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_ascii_frame(tcp, frame_data, frame_size, ...);
// Receive packet with dispatcher
acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(tcp, &callbacks);
// Or create WebSocket client transport
acip_transport_t *ws = acip_websocket_client_transport_create("ws://localhost:27225", crypto_ctx);
asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_ascii_frame(ws, frame_data, frame_size, ...);
// Cleanup
acip_transport_destroy(tcp);
acip_transport_destroy(ws);

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport owns its connection state (socket, pointers, etc.)
  • send() does NOT take ownership of data (caller must keep it valid)
  • recv() allocates buffer, caller must free via out_allocated_buffer
  • destroy() cleans up all transport resources and frees transport structure

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Version

1.0 (Transport API)

Definition in file transport.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum acip_transport_type_t

Transport type enumeration. Identifies which transport implementation is being used. Useful for debugging and conditional logic.

Enumerator

ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP
Raw TCP socket.
ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET
WebSocket over TCP.
ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC
WebRTC DataChannel (P2P).
ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP
HTTP long-polling (future).
ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC
QUIC/UDP (future).

Definition at line 93 of file transport.h.

93              {
94   ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP = 1,       
95   ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET = 2, 
96   ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC = 3,    
97   ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP = 4,      
98   ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC = 5       
99 } acip_transport_type_t;










Function
  Documentation




acip_transport_t * acip_tcp_transport_create (const char *
  name, socket_t sockfd, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create TCP transport from existing socket.


Parameters


name Debug name for tracking this transport
  (required, e.g., "transport_client_5")


sockfd Connected socket descriptor


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context (may be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Note


Caller retains socket ownership (transport doesn't close
  on destroy)


Definition at line 344 of file tcp/transport.c.


344                                                                                                              {
345   if (!name) {
346     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
347     return NULL;
348   }
349 
350   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
351     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ INVALID_SOCKET: sockfd=%d", sockfd);
352     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
353     return NULL;
354   }
355 
356   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 CREATE_START: sockfd=%d, crypto=%s", sockfd, crypto_ctx ? "yes" : "no");
357 
358   // Allocate transport structure
359   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
360   if (!transport) {
361     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ ALLOC_TRANSPORT_FAILED: size=%zu", sizeof(acip_transport_t));
362     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate TCP transport");
363     return NULL;
364   }
365 
366   log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TRANSPORT_ALLOCATED: transport=%p", (void *)transport);
367 
368   // Allocate TCP-specific data
369   tcp_transport_data_t *tcp_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(tcp_transport_data_t), tcp_transport_data_t *);
370   if (!tcp_data) {
371     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ ALLOC_TCP_DATA_FAILED: size=%zu", sizeof(tcp_transport_data_t));
372     SAFE_FREE(transport);
373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate TCP transport data");
374     return NULL;
375   }
376 
377   log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_DATA_ALLOCATED: tcp_data=%p", (void *)tcp_data);
378 
379   // Initialize TCP data
380   tcp_data->sockfd = sockfd;
381   tcp_data->is_connected = true;
382 
383   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent sends from multiple threads
384   int mutex_result = mutex_init(&tcp_data->send_mutex, "tcp_send");
385   if (mutex_result != 0) {
386     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ MUTEX_INIT_FAILED: result=%d", mutex_result);
387     SAFE_FREE(tcp_data);
388     SAFE_FREE(transport);
389     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
390     return NULL;
391   }
392 
393   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_DATA_INITIALIZED: sockfd=%d, is_connected=true, send_mutex=initialized", sockfd);
394 
395   // Enable TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle's algorithm
396   // This ensures small packets are sent immediately instead of being buffered
397   int nodelay = 1;
398   if (setsockopt(sockfd, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY, (const char *)&nodelay, sizeof(nodelay)) < 0) {
399     log_warn("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ⚠️  TCP_NODELAY_FAILED: sockfd=%d, %s", sockfd, SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
400     // Continue anyway - this is not fatal
401   } else {
402     log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_NODELAY_SET: sockfd=%d", sockfd);
403   }
404 
405   // Initialize transport
406   transport->methods = &tcp_methods;
407   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
408   transport->impl_data = tcp_data;
409 
410   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ✅ CREATE_COMPLETE: transport=%p, sockfd=%d, is_connected=true, crypto=%s",
411            (void *)transport, sockfd, crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
412 
413   // Register transport
414   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
415 
416   // Register impl_data with transport as parent
417   NAMED_REGISTER(tcp_data, "impl", "tcp_impl", "0x%tx", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
418 
419   return transport;
420 }






References acip_transport::crypto_ctx, errno,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    acip_transport::impl_data, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    tcp_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_STRERROR, tcp_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    SET_ERRNO, and tcp_transport_data_t::sockfd.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), add_client(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), server_connection_establish(), and
    server_main().






void
  acip_transport_destroy (acip_transport_t * transport)


Destroy transport and free all resources.


Parameters


transport Transport to destroy (may be NULL)


Note


Calls close() first if still connected



Frees the transport structure itself




Safe to pass NULL




Definition at line 426 of file tcp/transport.c.


426                                                          {
427   if (!transport) {
428     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ⚠️  NULL_TRANSPORT: nothing to destroy");
429     return;
430   }
431 
432   log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 DESTROY_START: transport=%p, impl_data=%p", (void *)transport, transport->impl_data);
433 
434   // Get type before we destroy for logging
435   acip_transport_type_t type = 0;
436   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->get_type) {
437     type = transport->methods->get_type(transport);
438     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 📋 TRANSPORT_TYPE: type=%d (1=TCP, 2=WebSocket, 3=WebRTC, ...)", type);
439   }
440 
441   // Close if still connected
442   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->close && transport->methods->is_connected &&
443       transport->methods->is_connected(transport)) {
444     log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 STILL_CONNECTED: calling close() first (type=%d)", type);
445     asciichat_error_t close_result = transport->methods->close(transport);
446     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 CLOSE_CALLED: result=%d", close_result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? -1 : 0);
447   } else {
448     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ ALREADY_CLOSED: skipping close (type=%d)", type);
449   }
450 
451   // Call custom destroy implementation if provided
452   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->destroy_impl) {
453     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔧 CALLING_DESTROY_IMPL: type=%d", type);
454     transport->methods->destroy_impl(transport);
455     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ DESTROY_IMPL_COMPLETE: type=%d", type);
456   } else {
457     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ⏭️  NO_CUSTOM_DESTROY: type=%d (using generic cleanup)", type);
458   }
459 
460   // Free implementation data
461   if (transport->impl_data) {
462     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🗑️  FREEING_IMPL_DATA: %p", transport->impl_data);
463     NAMED_UNREGISTER(transport->impl_data);
464     SAFE_FREE(transport->impl_data);
465     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ IMPL_DATA_FREED");
466   }
467 
468   // Free transport structure
469   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🗑️  FREEING_TRANSPORT: %p", (void *)transport);
470   SAFE_FREE(transport);
471 
472   log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ DESTROY_COMPLETE: type=%d", type);
473 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    acip_transport_methods_t::close,
    acip_transport_methods_t::destroy_impl,
    acip_transport_methods_t::get_type, acip_transport::impl_data,
    acip_transport_methods_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, log_warn, acip_transport::methods,
    NAMED_UNREGISTER, and SAFE_FREE.

    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_context_cleanup(),
    discovery_session_destroy(), remove_client(),
    server_connection_close(), server_connection_establish(),
    server_connection_set_transport(), and server_main().






acip_transport_t * acip_webrtc_transport_create (struct
  webrtc_peer_connection * peer_conn, struct webrtc_data_channel *
  data_channel, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Definition at line 368 of file
  webrtc/transport.c.


369                                                                              {
370   if (!peer_conn || !data_channel) {
371     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "peer_conn and data_channel are required");
372     return NULL;
373   }
374 
375   // Allocate transport structure
376   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
377   if (!transport) {
378     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebRTC transport");
379     return NULL;
380   }
381 
382   // Allocate WebRTC-specific data
383   webrtc_transport_data_t *wrtc_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(webrtc_transport_data_t), webrtc_transport_data_t *);
384   if (!wrtc_data) {
385     SAFE_FREE(transport);
386     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebRTC transport data");
387     return NULL;
388   }
389 
390   // Create receive queue
391   wrtc_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(webrtc_recv_msg_t), WEBRTC_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
392   if (!wrtc_data->recv_queue) {
393     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
394     SAFE_FREE(transport);
395     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
396     return NULL;
397   }
398 
399   // Initialize synchronization primitives
400   if (mutex_init(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex, "webrtc_queue") != 0) {
401     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
402     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
403     SAFE_FREE(transport);
404     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize queue mutex");
405     return NULL;
406   }
407 
408   if (cond_init(&wrtc_data->queue_cond, "queue") != 0) {
409     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
410     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
411     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
412     SAFE_FREE(transport);
413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize queue condition variable");
414     return NULL;
415   }
416 
417   if (mutex_init(&wrtc_data->state_mutex, "webrtc_state") != 0) {
418     cond_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_cond);
419     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
420     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
421     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
422     SAFE_FREE(transport);
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
424     return NULL;
425   }
426 
427   // Initialize WebRTC data
428   wrtc_data->peer_conn = peer_conn;
429   wrtc_data->data_channel = data_channel;
430   wrtc_data->is_connected = false; // Will be set to true in on_open callback
431 
432   // Register DataChannel callbacks
433   webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t callbacks = {
434       .on_open = webrtc_on_open,
435       .on_close = webrtc_on_close,
436       .on_error = webrtc_on_error,
437       .on_message = webrtc_on_message,
438       .user_data = wrtc_data,
439   };
440 
441   asciichat_error_t result = webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks(data_channel, &callbacks);
442   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
443     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
444     cond_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_cond);
445     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
446     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
447     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
448     SAFE_FREE(transport);
449     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to set DataChannel callbacks");
450     return NULL;
451   }
452 
453   // IMPORTANT: The transport is always created from peer_manager's on_datachannel_open callback,
454   // which means the DataChannel is ALREADY OPEN when we get here. However, by setting our own
455   // callbacks above (webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks), we replaced the callbacks that would
456   // have set dc->is_open=true. So we need to manually mark both the transport AND the DataChannel
457   // as open/connected now.
458   //
459   // We cannot rely on webrtc_on_open being called later because:
460   // 1. The DataChannel is already open
461   // 2. libdatachannel won't fire the open event again
462   // 3. Setting callbacks after open doesn't trigger a retroactive open event
463 
464   // Mark DataChannel as open (needed for webrtc_datachannel_send() check)
465   webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state(data_channel, true);
466 
467   // Mark transport as connected
468   mutex_lock(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
469   wrtc_data->is_connected = true;
470   mutex_unlock(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
471   log_debug("Transport and DataChannel marked as connected/open (already open from peer_manager callback)");
472 
473   // Initialize transport
474   transport->methods = &webrtc_methods;
475   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
476   transport->impl_data = wrtc_data;
477 
478   log_info("Created WebRTC transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
479 
480   // Register WebRTC transport implementation data with transport as parent
481   NAMED_REGISTER(wrtc_data, "impl", "webrtc_impl", "0x%tx", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
482 
483   return transport;
484 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, cond_destroy(),
    cond_init(), acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::data_channel, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NAMED_REGISTER, webrtc_transport_data_t::peer_conn,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::queue_cond,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::queue_mutex,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, webrtc_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks(),
    webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state(), and
    WEBRTC_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE.






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create
  (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket client transport. Connects to a WebSocket server
    at the specified URL and creates a transport for sending/receiving ACIP
    packets over the WebSocket connection.




Parameters


name Debug name for tracking this transport
  (required, e.g., "transport_websocket_client")


url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225" or
  "wss://example.com/path")


crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on failure


Note


The URL must include the protocol (ws:// or wss://)



Port defaults to 80 for ws:// and 443 for wss:// if not
  specified




Connection is established synchronously during creation




Parameters


url WebSocket URL (e.g.,
  "ws://localhost:27225")


crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on failure


Definition at line 1365 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1366                                                                                        {
1367   if (!name) {
1368     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1369     return NULL;
1370   }
1371 
1372   if (!url) {
1373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1374     return NULL;
1375   }
1376 
1377   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1378   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1379   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1380   if (!protocol_end) {
1381     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1382     return NULL;
1383   }
1384 
1385   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1386   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1387 
1388   // Find port (if specified)
1389   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1390   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1391 
1392   char host[256] = {0};
1393   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1394   char path[256] = "/";
1395 
1396   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1397     // Port is specified
1398     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1399     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1400       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1401       return NULL;
1402     }
1403     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1404     host[host_len] = '\0';
1405 
1406     // Extract port
1407     port = atoi(port_start + 1);
1408     if (port <= 0 || port > 65535) {
1409       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1410       return NULL;
1411     }
1412   } else {
1413     // No port specified, use default
1414     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1415     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1416       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1417       return NULL;
1418     }
1419     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1420     host[host_len] = '\0';
1421   }
1422 
1423   // Extract path
1424   if (path_start) {
1425     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1426     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1427   }
1428 
1429   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1430 
1431   // Allocate transport structure
1432   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1433   if (!transport) {
1434     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1435     return NULL;
1436   }
1437 
1438   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1439   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1440       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1441   if (!ws_data) {
1442     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1443     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1444     return NULL;
1445   }
1446 
1447   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1448   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1449 
1450   // Create receive queue
1451   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1452   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1453     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1454     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1455     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1456     return NULL;
1457   }
1458 
1459   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1460   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1461   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1462     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1463     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1464     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1465     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1466     return NULL;
1467   }
1468 
1469   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1470   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1471   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1472   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1473 
1474   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1475     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1476     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1477     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1478     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1479     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1480     return NULL;
1481   }
1482 
1483   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1484     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1485     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1486     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1487     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1488     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1489     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1490     return NULL;
1491   }
1492 
1493   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1494     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1495     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1496     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1497     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1498     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1499     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1500     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1501     return NULL;
1502   }
1503 
1504   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1505     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1506     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1507     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1508     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1509     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1510     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1511     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1512     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1513     return NULL;
1514   }
1515 
1516   // Allocate initial send buffer
1517   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1518   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1519   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1520     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1521     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1522     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1523     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1524     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1525     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1526     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1527     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1528     return NULL;
1529   }
1530 
1531   // Create libwebsockets context
1532   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1533   // NOTE: Define ONLY "acip" protocol for WebSocket connections
1534   // libwebsockets provides built-in "h1" and "http" protocols for WebSocket upgrade handshake.
1535   // When local_protocol_name="h1" and protocol="acip" are specified:
1536   // 1. libwebsockets uses built-in h1 (HTTP/1.1) for initial connection
1537   // 2. HTTP upgrade happens automatically (101 Switching Protocols)
1538   // 3. Connection switches to "acip" protocol using our custom callback
1539   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1540       {
1541           "acip", // ACIP protocol - used after WebSocket upgrade handshake completes
1542           websocket_callback,
1543           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1544           524288, // RX buffer size
1545           0,      // ID
1546           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1547           524288  // TX packet size
1548       },
1549       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1550   };
1551 
1552   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1553   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1554   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1555   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1556   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1557   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1558   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1559 
1560   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1561   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1562   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1563   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1564   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1565   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1566   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1567   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1568   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1569   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1570 
1571   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1572   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1573   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1574   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1575 
1576   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1577   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1578   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1579       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1580       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1581   };
1582   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1583 
1584   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1585   lws_log_init_client();
1586 
1587   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1588 
1589   if (!ws_data->context) {
1590     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1591     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1592     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1593     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1594     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1595     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1596     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1597     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1598     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1599     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1600     return NULL;
1601   }
1602 
1603   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1604   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1605   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1606   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1607   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1608 
1609   // Connect to WebSocket server
1610   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1611   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1612   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1613   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1614   connect_info.address = host;
1615   connect_info.port = port;
1616   connect_info.path = path;
1617   connect_info.host = host;
1618   connect_info.origin = host;
1619   // Use h1 (HTTP/1.1) protocol for WebSocket upgrade with acip as the subprotocol
1620   // - local_protocol_name="h1": libwebsockets' built-in HTTP/1.1 handler for WebSocket upgrade
1621   // - protocol="acip": Request this subprotocol from the remote server (Sec-WebSocket-Protocol header)
1622   // libwebsockets internally uses h1 regardless of the protocol name, so we use h1 directly
1623   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "h1"; // libwebsockets built-in HTTP/1.1
1624   connect_info.protocol = "acip";          // Remote subprotocol to request from server
1625   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1626   connect_info.ssl_connection =
1627       use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1628   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1629 
1630   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1631   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1632   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1633   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1634     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1635     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1636     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1637     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1638     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1639     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1640     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1641     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1642     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1643     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1644     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1645     return NULL;
1646   }
1647 
1648   // Initialize transport
1649   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1650   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1651   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1652 
1653   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1654   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1655   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1656   ws_data->service_running = true;
1657   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1658     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1659     ws_data->service_running = false;
1660     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1661     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1662     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1663     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1664     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1665     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1666     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1667     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1668     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1669     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1670     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1671     return NULL;
1672   }
1673   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1674 
1675   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1676   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1677   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1678   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1679   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1680   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1681 
1682   // Register transport
1683   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1684 
1685   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1686   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1687 
1688   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1689   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1690   return transport;
1691 }






References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::connection_failed,
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_NETWORK, acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_destroying, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, lws_log_init_client(),
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_running,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_thread, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming
  clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1703 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1704                                                                                        {
1705   if (!name) {
1706     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1707     return NULL;
1708   }
1709 
1710   if (!wsi) {
1711     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1712     return NULL;
1713   }
1714 
1715   // Allocate transport structure
1716   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1717   if (!transport) {
1718     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1719     return NULL;
1720   }
1721 
1722   // Allocate transport-specific data
1723   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1724       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1725   if (!ws_data) {
1726     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1727     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1728     return NULL;
1729   }
1730 
1731   // Initialize receive queue
1732   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1733   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1734     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1735     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1736     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1737     return NULL;
1738   }
1739 
1740   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1741   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1742   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1743     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1744     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1745     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1746     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1747     return NULL;
1748   }
1749 
1750   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1751   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1752   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1753   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1754   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1755     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1756     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1757     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1758     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1759     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1760     return NULL;
1761   }
1762 
1763   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1764   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1765   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1766   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1767   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1768   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1769 
1770   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1771     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1772     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1773     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1774     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1775     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1776     return NULL;
1777   }
1778 
1779   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1780     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1781     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1782     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1783     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1784     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1785     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1786     return NULL;
1787   }
1788 
1789   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1790     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1791     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1792     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1793     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1794     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1795     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1796     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1797     return NULL;
1798   }
1799 
1800   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1801     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1802     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1803     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1804     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1805     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1806     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1807     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1808     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1809     return NULL;
1810   }
1811 
1812   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1813     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1814     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1815     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1816     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1817     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1818     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1819     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1820     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1821     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1822     return NULL;
1823   }
1824 
1825   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1826     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1827     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1828     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1829     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1830     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1831     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1832     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1833     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1834     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1835     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1836     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1837     return NULL;
1838   }
1839 
1840   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1841   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1842   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1843   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1844     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1845     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1846     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1847     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1848     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1849     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1850     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1851     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1852     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1853     return NULL;
1854   }
1855   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1856 
1857   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1858   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1859   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1860   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1861   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1862   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1863            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1864   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1865 
1866   // Initialize transport
1867   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1868   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1869   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1870 
1871   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1872 
1873   // Register transport
1874   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1875 
1876   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1877   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1878 
1879   return transport;
1880 }






References cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT,
    NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.7
    ascii-chat
